description_long: This rifle features a classic wooden stock and foregrip, with a dark metallic receiver and barrel assembly. The stock is straight and fixed, extending smoothly from the rear to the trigger guard, and the foregrip is similarly wooden, providing a traditional look. The barrel is relatively short and standard, ending with a simple muzzle and no visible attachments. The rifle is equipped with basic iron sights, with a rear notch and a front post, suitable for close to medium-range targeting. The magazine is a detachable box type, protruding from the bottom just ahead of the trigger guard. The overall design is utilitarian, with a blend of wood and metal materials, and is typical of mid-20th-century military carbines.
description: A classic rifle with a wooden stock and foregrip, dark metal receiver and barrel, iron sights, and a detachable box magazine.
description_view_0: Front view showing the muzzle, front sight post, and the narrow profile of the barrel and stock.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the wooden stock, receiver, trigger, and detachable box magazine.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the buttstock and the alignment of the iron sights along the barrel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden stock, receiver, and magazine from the other side.
